steps.title: Find the factor pair
steps.content: For $x^2+5x-36$, look for two numbers that multiply to $-36$ and add to $5$. The pair is $-4$ and $9$.

steps.title: Write the factors
steps.content:
Use those two numbers as the constants in the binomials.
$$x^2+5x-36=(x-4)(x+9)$$

steps.title: Match the expression
steps.content: The equivalent factored form is $(x-4)(x+9)$. The answer is B.
